data.body:
## Description

**What**: Regression test suite reproducing at least 3 recent workflow bug fixes as E2E test cases
**Why**: Proves the E2E suite catches the category of bugs it was designed to prevent — not just theoretical coverage
**Scope**:
- IN: Select 3+ bugs from the 34 recent workflow fix commits, write E2E tests that reproduce each, verify the test fails when the fix is reverted
- OUT: Bugs that require LLM interaction to reproduce

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, at least 3 E2E test cases exist that each reproduce a specific recent workflow bug (identified by ticket ID)
  ← Satisfies: "At least 3 of the 34 recent workflow bug fixes are reproducible as E2E test cases"
- When this story is complete, each regression test is documented with which bug it reproduces and how to verify it catches the regression (revert the fix → test fails)
  ← Satisfies: "bugs more reliably caught and diagnosed by tests"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Bug selection should prioritize workflow/multi-script interaction bugs — the dominant category
